title: Iranian Military Shows It Knows How to Adapt, U.S. Officials Say
summary: Iran appears to be targeting what it views as American vulnerabilities, including air defenses meant to guard troops and assets in the region.

title: How Trump and His Advisers Miscalculated Iran’s Response to War
summary: In the lead-up to the U.S.-Israeli attack, President Trump downplayed the risks to the energy markets as a short-term concern that should not overshadow the mission to decapitate the Iranian regime.

title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Was Wounded Early in the War, Iranian and Israeli Officials Say
summary: Officials say Mojtaba Khamenei’s legs were hurt, but the circumstances as well as the extent of his injuries were unclear. He has remained out of view since being announced as leader three days ago.

title: Bondi Is Said to Move to Military Housing Because of Threats
summary: The attorney general relocated from a Washington apartment to a base in the area within the past month, according to people familiar with the situation.

title: Senate Moves Toward Passing Sweeping Housing Bill, but Challenges Lie Ahead
summary: The legislation’s progress is all the more surprising because it addresses an issue that is shaping up to be the main battleground of the midterm elections: affordability. The effort could still stall.

title: Trump Administration to Restart Global Entry Program
summary: The program for people traveling internationally, set to come back online at 5 a.m. Wednesday, had been paused amid the shutdown of the Homeland Security Department.

title: Voting Company Smartmatic Says It’s a Target of Vindictive Prosecution by Trump
summary: The voting technology company’s new court filing signals how far-reaching the legal fights over elections will be in 2026.

title: Clayton Fuller and Shawn Harris Advance to Runoff in Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Georgia District
summary: Clayton Fuller will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in an April runoff. Mr. Fuller’s win over a flashier Republican showed the power of the president’s support.

title: Bennie Thompson Defeats Young Challenger in Mississippi Primary
summary: The victory by Mr. Thompson, the state’s longest-serving Black Democrat, offers a counterpoint to the broader push nationwide for younger leadership in Congress.

title: Culture at Columbia Shielded Sexual Assault by Physician, Report Finds
summary: Two doctors affiliated with the university and NewYork-Presbyterian Hospital stepped down after an external report found that the administrators mishandled allegations of abuse against Robert Hadden.

title: Ukraine Can Now Manufacture ‘China-Free’ Drones
summary: The country has prioritized self-sufficiency in producing a crucial battlefield weapon, though weaning itself fully off cheaper Chinese components is difficult.

title: San Francisco Bay Area Residents Weigh Possibility of BART Reductions
summary: The Bay Area Rapid Transit system was once so successful, it could rely mostly on riders to sustain itself. But the pandemic dealt BART an unusually heavy blow.

title: Noma Loses American Express and Blackbird Sponsors For Los Angeles Dinners
summary: Two companies have withdrawn from a series of dinners in Los Angeles after The New York Times reported allegations that René Redzepi attacked workers.

title: Alberto Carvalho, Suspended LAUSD Chief, Denies Wrongdoing
summary: Through his lawyers, Alberto Carvalho, who was put on leave after the F.B.I. raided his home and office, said that his actions were appropriate but that he would respect the investigative process.

title: Who Are the Men Accused of Bringing Homemade Bombs to Gracie Mansion?
summary: A high school senior and a recent graduate, both from the Philadelphia suburbs, were charged by federal prosecutors with trying to support a terrorist group.

title: How Does This End? Four Scenarios for What Comes Next With Iran.
summary: The best hope? Weaken the regime’s ability to lash out and resist an uprising.

title: Leave the Kurds Out of It
summary: The United States and Israel have considered using Iranian Kurds as a ground force.

title: Tornadoes Reported Across the Midwest as Violent Storms Move Through
summary: The Weather Service issued several of its most urgent alerts on Tuesday night.

title: Matt Snell Dies at 84; Carried Jets to Stunning Upset in Super Bowl III
summary: His pounding runs for the underdog New York team against the Baltimore Colts secured a pivotal win for the American Football League.

title: Bill Kurtis to Leave NPR’s ‘Wait Wait … Don’t Tell Me!’
summary: As the irreverent quiz show’s judge and scorekeeper, Kurtis, a former news anchor known for his rich voice, has helped find comedy in current events since 2014.

title: Meta Acquires Moltbook, the Social Network Just for A.I. Bots
summary: Matt Schlicht, the site’s creator, helped kick off Silicon Valley’s obsession with artificial intelligence agents. Two months later, he is joining the Meta Superintelligence Lab.
